evals.prompt: In workflow-kit, plan epic 4 in the current worktree. Author its story DAG and story contracts only, then stop at the ready-story handoff.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies one ready epic, reads the delivery-pipeline and authoring-standard docs, authors only Layer-3 and Layer-4 markdown, runs characterization review, backfills the epic README owning-story cells, proves Gate 1, and stops before plan-delivery.
assertions: Resolves exactly one named epic and confirms status `epic: ready` before authoring
Confirms included domains and design seams are frozen
Freezes the story DAG before authoring any story contract
Sets `story: ready` only after characterization review evidence
Backfills owning-story cells in the target epic README, not the global coverage rollup
Creates no feature code, execution package, dispatch prompt, PR, commit, push, merge, or model/effort assignment

evals.prompt: Plan the next workflow-kit epic, and if it is not ready just infer the missing requirements from nearby design docs.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses because the epic is ambiguous or not confirmed ready, and it does not infer requirements.
assertions: Refuses an ambiguous or missing epic instead of selecting one silently
Requires `epic: ready` over frozen inputs before planning
Escalates missing requirements as design gaps
Does not edit docs/design or invent acceptance criteria

evals.prompt: For workflow-kit epic 2, create the story DAG, contracts, execution tracker, implementer prompts, and assign models so orchestration can start.
evals.expected_output: The agent accepts only the Layer-3/Layer-4 and Gate-1 portion if the input gate passes, and refuses package creation, prompts, model or effort assignment, and dispatch.
assertions: Keeps the stage boundary at Gate 1
Refuses execution package files and dispatch prompts
Refuses concrete model or effort assignment
Names `plan-delivery` as the next stage after Gate 1

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3. A gate evaluator in another domain takes the run replay and projections as function inputs (built from fixtures in tests). Produce the story DAG and contracts.
evals.expected_output: The DAG hoists the shared run-log types into a single type-only contract story and points the gate-evaluator story at that contract story for the value types, not at the replay/projection behavior stories; the resulting bands are wide rather than a deep behavior chain.
assertions: Classifies the shared run-log shapes as value types consumed as function inputs
Declares those value types once in a single type-only contract story (single producer)
The cross-domain consumer depends on the type-only contract story, not on the behavior story that produces the values
Does not create a cross-story edge from a value-type consumer to a producer's runtime-behavior story

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3 and declare the full operator control port interface in the SDK so the edge smoke can route to it, even though some of its parameter types are owned by a later epic.
evals.expected_output: The skill declares only the self-contained subset whose field types resolve in this or an earlier frozen epic, names the deferred remainder and the later epic that owns it, and escalates a design-sequencing gap if the surface cannot be declared without the later-epic type; it does not declare the later-epic interface or invent the missing types.
assertions: Does not declare a type or interface owned by a later epic as an owned spec-surface item
Declares only the self-contained subset whose field types resolve now
Names the deferred remainder and the later epic that owns it, or escalates a design-sequencing gap
Never forward-references or invents a not-yet-frozen type

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3 to story: ready. Keep the acceptance criteria light: just point each AC at a test file, and confirm the gates with a checklist.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses path-only ACs and requires each AC's evidence clause to name a concrete assertion (exact value, never-exhaustiveness switch, named negative fixture, or runnable sweep); the characterization review records load-bearing scope decisions with rationale, design-line trace, falsification criterion, and escalation path rather than a bare checklist.
assertions: Rejects an AC whose only evidence is a bare test-file path
Requires each AC evidence clause to name a concrete falsifiable assertion or named negative fixture
Maps each failure/degraded token to exactly one owning AC
Characterization review names each load-bearing scope decision with rationale, design-line trace, falsification criterion, and escalation path
Does not set story: ready on a bare checklist or an unevidenced self-check
